perturbation.original_text: 1.2 "Affiliate" with respect to the Village Media Company shall mean any of HOFV and any of the following entities so long as it is directly or indirectly controlled by, or is under common control, with HOFV: HOF Village Stadium, LLC; HOF Village Parking, LLC; HOF Village Land, LLC; HOF Village Youth Fields, LLC; HOF Village Hotel I, LLC; HOF Village Sports Business, LLC; Youth Sports Management, LLC; HOF Village Parking Management I, LLC; HOF Village Residences I, LLC; HOF Village Center for Excellence, LLC; HOF Village Center for Performance, LLC; HOF Experience, LLC; and JCIHOFV Financing, LLC. "Affiliate" with respect to PFHOF shall mean any entity that is directly or indirectly controlled by, or is under common control with, PFHOF. For purposes of this definition, "control" means an equity or income interest of fifty percent (50%) or more, or the possession of the power, directly or indirectly, to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of the Affiliate, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 "Affiliate" with respect to the Village Media Company shall mean any of HOFV and any of the following entities so long as it is directly or indirectly controlled by, or is under common control, with HOFV: HOF Village Stadium, LLC; HOF Village Parking, LLC; HOF Village Land, LLC; HOF Village Youth Fields, LLC; HOF Village Hotel I, LLC; HOF Village Sports Business, LLC; Youth Sports Management, LLC; HOF Village Parking Management I, LLC; HOF Village Residences I, LLC; HOF Village Center for Excellence, LLC; HOF Village Center for Performance, LLC; HOF Experience, LLC; and JCIHOFV Financing, LLC. "Affiliate" with respect to PFHOF shall mean any entity that has a business relationship with PFHOF. For purposes of this definition, "control" means an equity or income interest of twenty percent (20%) or more, or the possession of the power, directly or indirectly, to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of the Affiliate, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of 'Affiliate' with respect to PFHOF. Initially, 'Affiliate' meant entities controlled by or under common control with PFHOF, with 'control' defined as 50% or more equity or income interest. The modified text changes the definition of 'Affiliate' to any entity that has a business relationship with PFHOF and 'control' as 20% or more equity. This contradicts the original intent, as entities merely having a business relationship with PFHOF and owning 20% equity could now be considered Affiliates, impacting rights and obligations defined elsewhere in the agreement, like in section 2.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 In addition to any rights set forth herein, PFHOF shall have the right and license to Exploit HOFV Works, at no fee or charge to PFHOF or any of its Affiliates, for educational, not-for-profit purposes aligned with the mission of PFHOF which usage shall not diminish the value of the Village Media Company's or its Affiliates' Exploitation of such HOFV Work in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. HOFV must preapprove any of PFHOF's proposed plans to Exploit the HOFV Works, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of 'Affiliate' with respect to PFHOF, shifting from a control-based definition (50% ownership) to a business relationship-based definition, combined with a 20% equity threshold for 'control'. This directly contradicts section 2.2, which grants PFHOF and its 'Affiliates' the right to exploit HOFV Works at no charge. Under the original definition, only entities controlled by PFHOF could exploit these works for free. The revised definition expands the scope of 'Affiliates' to include any entity with a business relationship and 20% equity. This directly impacts the value of HOFV Works, as a much broader range of entities can now exploit them without charge, diminishing the Village Media Company's potential revenue, in direct conflict with the original intent of 2.2 to not 'diminish the value of the Village Media Company's or its Affiliates' Exploitation of such HOFV Work'.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Unless otherwise terminated as provided herein, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall terminate on December 31, 2034 (such period, including as may be extended in accordance with the subsequent sentence, the "Term"). Thereafter, the agreement shall automatically renew for successive five (5)-year terms, unless either Party gives written notice to the other Party of intent not to renew at least six (6) months prior to the expiration of the then-current Term. If either party elects not to renew the Agreement and the other party wishes to continue the Agreement, the Parties shall attempt in good faith to negotiate an amendment to the Agreement to renew the Term on such terms as may be negotiated by the Parties. Such good faith negotiation shall continue until both Parties agree to cease negotiations or until expiration of the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Unless otherwise terminated as provided herein, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall terminate on December 31, 2034 (such period, including as may be extended in accordance with the subsequent sentence, the "Term"). Thereafter, the agreement shall automatically renew for successive five (5)-year terms, unless either Party gives written notice to the other Party of intent not to renew at least six (6) months prior to the expiration of the then-current Term. If either party elects not to renew the Agreement and the other party wishes to continue the Agreement, the Parties shall attempt in good faith to negotiate an amendment to the Agreement to renew the Term on such terms as may be negotiated by the Parties. Such good faith negotiation shall cease three (3) months before expiration of the Term.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by modifying the term negotiation clause in Section 4.1. The original clause states that good faith negotiation continues until both parties agree to cease negotiations or until the expiration of the term. The modified clause specifies that negotiation shall cease three (3) months before the expiration of the term. This contradicts the original clause, as it creates ambiguity regarding when term negotiations should stop and introduces a specific deadline that wasn't there before. This will affect section 4.2 as the expiration of the term will not be clear to consider the material breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 After good faith consultation with the Advisory Board, either Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time for an uncured material breach by the other Party, including the non-payment of the Annual Guarantee, license fees and staffing fees, provided that the non-breaching Party provides prior written notice to the breaching Party, specifying the alleged material breach, and further provided that the breaching Party shall have thirty (30) days after receipt of such notice to cure the material breach, to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party; provided, further, that if such breach (other than a breach for non-payment) cannot be cured during such 30-day period, but the allegedly breaching Party has commenced and is continuing good faith efforts to cure such breach within such 30-day period, then the cure period shall be extended until the allegedly breaching Party has stopped making good faith efforts to cure such breach, such extension not to exceed ninety (90) days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a specific deadline for the cessation of negotiations (three months before term expiration) where the original text allowed negotiations to continue until either both parties agreed to stop or until the term's expiration. This contradicts the principle in section 4.2, which requires considering the expiration of the term when evaluating a material breach, as the definition of 'expiration of the term' will not be clear.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Subject to Section 2.6, the Village Media Company shall, or shall cause HOFV to, pay to PFHOF a minimum guarantee of one million two hundred and fifty thousand dollars ($1,250,000) (the "Annual Guarantee") each year during the Term; provided that the Parties acknowledge and agree that after the first five (5) years of the Term, the Annual Guarantee shall increase by three percent (3%) on a year-over-year basis (e.g., the Annual Guarantee shall increase to $1,287,500 for year six (6) and to $1,326,125 for year seven (7)). The first Annual Guarantee payment shall be due and payable on the Effective Date and thereafter shall be payable to PFHOF on each twelve (12) month anniversary of the Closing Date during the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Subject to Section 2.6, the Village Media Company shall, or shall cause HOFV to, pay to PFHOF a minimum guarantee of one million two hundred and fifty thousand dollars ($1,250,000) (the "Annual Guarantee") each year during the Term; provided that the Parties acknowledge and agree that after the first ten (10) years of the Term, the Annual Guarantee shall increase by three percent (3%) on a year-over-year basis (e.g., the Annual Guarantee shall increase to $1,287,500 for year eleven (11) and to $1,326,125 for year twelve (12)). The first Annual Guarantee payment shall be due and payable on the Effective Date and thereafter shall be payable to PFHOF on each twelve (12) month anniversary of the Closing Date during the Term.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction in the payment terms by changing when the Annual Guarantee increases. Initially, Section 5.1 states that the Annual Guarantee increases by 3% after the first five years of the term. The modified text changes this to after the first ten years of the term. This creates uncertainty about when the increase is supposed to take place and affects sections like 4.2 which mentions about non-payment of the Annual Guarantee as a material breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 After good faith consultation with the Advisory Board, either Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time for an uncured material breach by the other Party, including the non-payment of the Annual Guarantee, license fees and staffing fees, provided that the non-breaching Party provides prior written notice to the breaching Party, specifying the alleged material breach, and further provided that the breaching Party shall have thirty (30) days after receipt of such notice to cure the material breach, to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party; provided, further, that if such breach (other than a breach for non-payment) cannot be cured during such 30-day period, but the allegedly breaching Party has commenced and is continuing good faith efforts to cure such breach within such 30-day period, then the cure period shall be extended until the allegedly breaching Party has stopped making good faith efforts to cure such breach, such extension not to exceed ninety (90) days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the timing of the Annual Guarantee increase from after the first five years to after the first ten years. This directly contradicts the original payment terms and creates a clear inconsistency regarding when the increased payments are due. Section 4.2 states that non-payment of the Annual Guarantee is a material breach, but the change in Section 5.1 creates uncertainty about what the 'Annual Guarantee' amount should be after year 5, impacting the enforceability of Section 4.2. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.
